title: LCM-NanoPOTS workflow for spatial proteome
author: Yumi Kwon
materials: Find the target region on scanned image
Navigate to the region of interest (ROI).
Start the collection with CenterRoboLPC function.

An Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer (Thermo scientific) with FAIMS-pro interface operated in data-dependent acquisition mode was used for all analyses. Peptides were ionized by applying a voltage of 2,400 V.

steps.title: Before cut and collection
steps.instruction: Before cut and collection: Load DMSO as capturing media on the nanoPOTS chip

steps.title: Set the nanoPOTS robot to be ready (Temperature, humidity)
steps.instruction: Set the nanoPOTS robot to be ready (Temperature, humidity).

steps.title: Align the nanoPOTS chip
steps.instruction: Align the nanoPOTS chip.

steps.title: Set 100 µL of DMSO (Dimethyl...
steps.instruction: Set 100 µL of DMSO (Dimethyl sulfoxide) in the sample plate.

steps.title: Dispense 200 nL of DMSO into...
steps.instruction: Dispense 200 nL of DMSO into each well on the nanoPOTS chip

steps.title: Cover the chip with the glass...
steps.instruction: Cover the chip with the glass slide and cover with aluminum foil.

steps.title: Cut and tissue collection
steps.instruction: Cut and tissue collection

steps.title: Turn on the LCM (PALM MicroBeam)
steps.instruction: Turn on the LCM (PALM MicroBeam).

steps.title: Draw 4 × 9 grid lines...
steps.instruction: Draw 4 × 9 grid lines (1 voxel size= 2500 µm 2 ) on ROI.

steps.title: Load the laser setting (Speed 1,...
steps.instruction: Load the laser setting (Speed 1, Cut energy 40, LPC Energy delta15).

steps.title: Load the nanoPOTS chip on the collector (slide collector 48)
steps.instruction: Load the nanoPOTS chip on the collector (slide collector 48). Align A1 corner of slide collector and A2 well corner of the nanoPOTS chip.

steps.title: After collecting all voxels, check on...
steps.instruction: After collecting all voxels, check on the microscope if every DMSO droplet contains the tissue voxel.

steps.title: Remove the nanoPOTS chip carefully from...
steps.instruction: Remove the nanoPOTS chip carefully from thecollector and cover it with the glass slide.

steps.title: Set incubator temperature to 70 °C
steps.instruction: Set incubator temperature to 70 °C.

steps.title: Wrap with foil and store it...
steps.instruction: Wrap with foil and store it in -20 °C until analysis.

steps.title: After DMSO has evaporated, scan the...
steps.instruction: After DMSO has evaporated, scan the nanoPOTS chip and check if there is any missing well.

steps.title: After 15 min, unwrap the chip...
steps.instruction: After 15 min, unwrap the chip and place uncovered in incubator for 5 min intervals until DMSO has evaporate (~19 min).

steps.title: Place chips upside down (still wrapped)...
steps.instruction: Place chips upside down (still wrapped) into incubator for 15 min.

steps.title: Remove chips from incubator and place into nanoPOTS robot
steps.instruction: Remove chips from incubator and place into nanoPOTS robot.

steps.title: Dispense 50 nL of 5% formic...
steps.instruction: Dispense 50 nL of 5% formic acid in water into each well.

steps.title: Set the nanoPOTS robot to be ready (Temperature, humidity)
steps.instruction: Set the nanoPOTS robot to be ready (Temperature, humidity).

steps.title: Cover with a sterile glass slide,...
steps.instruction: Cover with a sterile glass slide, wrap with foil and store at -20 °C until analysis.

steps.title: Place the chip upside down in...
steps.instruction: Place the chip upside down in the humidity box in a zipper bag at 70 °C for 60 min.

steps.title: Cover chip with the glass slide...
steps.instruction: Cover chip with the glass slide and cover with aluminum foil.

steps.title: Dispense 200 nL of the extraction...
steps.instruction: Dispense 200 nL of the extraction buffer into each well on the nanoPOTS chip.

steps.title: Place 100 µL of extraction buffer...
steps.instruction: Place 100 µL of extraction buffer (1 mM TCEP (tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine), 0.1% DDM (n-Dodecy-ß-D-maltoside), 0.1 M HEPES (pH 8.5)) in the sample plate.

steps.title: Align the nanoPOTS chip
steps.instruction: Align the nanoPOTS chip.

steps.title: Set chip at RT and put it into nanoPOTS robot
steps.instruction: Set chip at RT and put it into nanoPOTS robot.

steps.title: Place the chip upside down in...
steps.instruction: Place the chip upside down in the dark for 30 min at RT.

steps.title: Cover chip with glass slide and wrap with foil
steps.instruction: Cover chip with glass slide and wrap with foil.

steps.title: Dispense 50 nL of alkylation buffer...
steps.instruction: Dispense 50 nL of alkylation buffer (10 mM CAA (2-chloroacetamide) in 0.1 M HEPES) into each well.

steps.title: Place 100 µL of alkylation buffer in the sample plate
steps.instruction: Place 100 µL of alkylation buffer in the sample plate.

steps.title: Set chip into nanoPOTS robot
steps.instruction: Set chip into nanoPOTS robot.

steps.title: Place the chip upside down in...
steps.instruction: Place the chip upside down in the humidity box for 10 h at 37 °C.

steps.title: Cover chip with a glass slide and cover with foil
steps.instruction: Cover chip with a glass slide and cover with foil.

steps.title: Dispense 50 nL of digestion buffer (0
steps.instruction: Dispense 50 nL of digestion buffer (0.01 ng/nL LysC, 0.04 ng/nL trypsin in 0.1 M HEPES) into each well.

steps.title: Place 100 µL of digestion buffer in the sample plate
steps.instruction: Place 100 µL of digestion buffer in the sample plate.

steps.title: Remove the chip from the nanoPOTS...
steps.instruction: Remove the chip from the nanoPOTS robot and place it into the desiccator until the droplets have evaporated.

steps.title: In-house assembled nanoPOTS autosampler with an...
steps.instruction: In-house assembled nanoPOTS autosampler with an in-house packed SPE column (100 μm i.d., 4 cm, 5 μm, 300 Å C18 material, Phenomenex) and an LC column (50 μm i.d., 25 cm, 1.7 μm, 190 Å C18 material, Waters) heated to 50 using AgileSleeve column heater (Analytical Sales and services, Inc., Flanders, NJ) was used for sample analysis.

steps.title: Samples were dissolved with Buffer A (0
steps.instruction: Samples were dissolved with Buffer A (0.1% formic acid in water) on the chip, then trapped on the SPE column for 5 min. After washing the peptides, samples were eluted at 100 nL/min and separated using a 60-min gradient from 8% to 35% Buffer B (0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile).

steps.title: The ionized peptides were fractionated by...
steps.instruction: The ionized peptides were fractionated by the FAIMSpro interface using a 3-CV (-45, -60, -75 V) method. Fractionated ions with a mass range 350-1600 m/z were scanned at 120,000 resolution with an IT of 118 ms and an AGC target of 1E6.

steps.title: For the pooled tissue samples for...
steps.instruction: For the pooled tissue samples for generating a spectral library, a single CV was used for each LC-MS run. Precursor ions with intensities > 1E4 were selected for fragmentation by 30% HCD and scanned in an Ion trap with an AGC of 2E5 and an IT of 86 ms.

steps.title: Use FragPipe (v 17
steps.instruction: Use FragPipe (v 17.1, MSFragger v3.4, Philosopher v 4.1.0) for processing (feature detection, database searching and protein/peptide quantification) of all raw files.

steps.title: For database search, use the latest Uniprot human database
steps.instruction: For database search, use the latest Uniprot human database.

steps.title: Fixed modification
steps.instruction: Fixed modification: carbamidomethylation of cysteine / Variable modification: Protein N-terminal acetylation, oxidation of methionine.

steps.title: Cleavage enzyme
steps.instruction: Cleavage enzyme: strict trypsin, peptide length: 7-50, max missed cleavage: 2, FDR 0.01

steps.title: For quantifications, use match between runs...
steps.instruction: For quantifications, use match between runs (MBR) and MaxLFQ embedded in the FragPipe (minimum ions:1; minimum scans: 3; m/z tolerance 10 ppm; RT tolerance 0.4 min; MBR FDR at ion level 0.05).
